preferred_recommendation_anchor: Task Scheduler recurring tick plus in-process PowerShell supervisor
primary_question: On a Windows workstation, what is the best bounded way to keep a local AI workflow running unattended without pretending the system is more reliable than it really is?
secondary_questions: What signals should count as proof that unattended work is actually progressing?
How should local runtime failures be surfaced without collapsing the entire loop?
intended_reader: technical sponsor deciding how to operate the system this week

context.current_pain_point: The project has many supporting documents, but the sponsor wants real product output and runtime trustworthiness.
context.known_constraints: No production access, no external publication without approval, local runtime should remain bounded and auditable.

definition_of_done.client_decision_after_reading: Choose the most credible unattended operating pattern for the current Windows machine.
definition_of_done.good_output: A short brief with options, a recommendation, tradeoffs, runtime signals, and immediate next actions.
definition_of_done.out_of_scope: Cloud orchestration, Linux service design, billing, and marketing language.

risk_notes.unclear_requirement: The sponsor wants both trustworthiness and speed, so the recommendation must stay bounded.
risk_notes.likely_blocker: Long-run status can look healthy when it is only partially verified.
